plan.action: Run the existing BATS tests for lib/agents.sh to identify which tests currently fail: bats tests/agents.bats

Fix agents_get_contract_field in lib/agents.sh if needed: ensure it only reads from frontmatter (between --- delimiters) and falls back to bare top-level fields for files without --- fences. The current grep-anywhere approach may return body content before frontmatter in edge cases.

Verify all six real Agents/*.md files (planner.md, reviewer.md, architect.md, tester.md, developer.md, deployment.md) pass agents_validate_contract. Each must have: role, inputs, outputs, constraints fields detectable by agents_get_contract_field. Check that none of the renamed files (planner vs planning, reviewer vs reviewing, tester vs test) have a mismatch that causes validation to fail.

Confirm agents_validate correctly uses AGENTS_REQUIRED_ROLES=(planner reviewer architect tester developer deployment) matching the renamed .md files, and that agents_load delegates to agents_validate with the correct path.

Confirm agents_compose_prompt substitution works end-to-end: the awk str_replace approach handles multi-line values for all five context keys (ticket, plan, adr, tech, tests). Verify against the test cases in tests/agents.bats lines 174-243.
Fix any test or implementation gaps found in steps 1-5, then re-run bats tests/agents.bats until all tests pass.

testing_recommendations: Run bats tests/agents.bats to establish current pass/fail baseline before making any changes
Pay attention to the 'real agent file passes contract validation' tests (lines 438-466) — these test actual Agents/*.md files on disk and will fail if any contract field is missing or not detectable

The test 'agents_get_contract_field does not return prompt body content' (line 316) validates frontmatter isolation — ensure the function returns the first (frontmatter) value, not body content, when both contain the same field name
Test agents_compose_prompt with multi-line ticket/plan values to ensure the awk ENVIRON substitution doesn't mangle newlines or special characters like & and \
Verify agents_load prints the expected '[agents] Registry loaded from: ...' message on success (tested implicitly by agents_load tests)
After any changes to lib/agents.sh, run the full test suite (bats tests/) to catch regressions in dependent modules
risks: Agents/tester.md has 'role: test' but the required role name is 'tester' — agents_validate_contract only checks field presence not value, so this passes, but if any downstream code resolves role by value it may break
Agents/reviewer.md has 'role: reviewing' not 'role: reviewer' — same concern as above

The grep-anywhere approach in agents_get_contract_field is fragile if any agent prompt body contains lines matching '^field:' pattern before the actual frontmatter field; this could cause incorrect values to be returned if the file structure is unusual

Real agent files use bare frontmatter (no --- delimiters) while test fixtures use --- fenced frontmatter — agents_get_prompt strips --- fenced content only, so for real files the role/inputs/outputs/constraints lines will be included in the composed prompt sent to Claude (may or may not be intentional)
